triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the policy limit for Tier A applicants.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider reducing the loan amount or increasing the deposit to meet the LTV requirement.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum LTV for Tier A applicants is 90%. The calculated LTV for this proposal is 91.11%, which exceeds this limit.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Policy Limit
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio slightly exceeds the policy limit for Tier A applicants.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider adjusting the loan terms or verifying income to ensure compliance.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 45% for Tier A applicants. The calculated DTI for this proposal is 45.83%, which is marginally above the limit.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified two medium-severity issues: the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio exceeds the policy limit for Tier A applicants, and the Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio is slightly above the maximum allowable threshold. These findings suggest that the proposal does not fully comply with the underwriting policy, requiring adjustments or further review.
